title: ACS predicates that subprocess whole-package `go test -race` flake under fleet contention — same class PR #340 fixed for the tier gate, unfixed at the predicate layer
why: Cycle-944 no-shipped on EGPS red_count=1: the sole red was TestC944_005_core_race_clean, a predicate that re-runs `go test -race internal/core` as a SUBPROCESS (37s) inside the ACS lane. Under 2-wide fleet contention this is the exact false-RED family PR #340 (3c5ed711) fixed for the integration-tier gate via env-scrub + flock-serialized retake — but the ACS predicate lane got neither. Such predicates ALSO duplicate the CI-parity tier gate (never_duplicate violation): the tier gate already proves core -race green; a predicate re-proving it adds only flake surface.
how_to_apply: Two-part class fix: (1) eval-quality pre-flight (evolve eval quality-check) rejects new predicates that shell out to whole-package go test / -race suites — predicates must assert specific behavior, not re-run gates; (2) for existing suite-runner predicates, acssuite serializes them through the same adapters/flock tier lock (independent wait) + scrubbed env used by integrationTierCheckDefault, and red->green on retake = WARN-absorb like the tier gate. Re-adjudicate cycle-944's deliverable after (it may be a lost-work salvage).
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-944/acs-verdict.json results[]: TestC944_005_core_race_clean exit_code=1 'go test -race ... exited 1' with orchestrator workspace-archive noise in stdout — contention signature
